{{Quote|Given that the parent never gave her consent, I don't know what the issue it is,''[sic]'' The Jedi say it's dangerous, but how can a six-month old child be dangerous?|Lomina Argo|<ref name="Jedi Watch" />}}
'''Lomina Argo''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[systems analyst]] on the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]<ref name="Jedi Watch" /> in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]],<ref name="Date">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|46|jediwatch/1337_1.html|Jedi Refuse to Hand Over Baby Ludi}} is set on the date 13:3:7, which corresponds to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], according to the reasoning [[Template:YearMathGRS|here]].</ref> Argo was interviewed by a [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] [[Journalist/Legends|journalist]] about the [["Baby Ludi" custody case]], which concerned the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] refusing to return a six-[[Standard month/Legends|month]]-old [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] Human, [[Aris-Del Wari|Ludi]], to her [[Parent/Legends|mother]], [[Jonava Billane]].<ref name="Jedi Watch" />

After hearing a statement by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Coleman Trebor/Legends|Coleman Trebor]], in which Trebor said that the infant's mind had been opened to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and that returning the [[Youngling/Legends|child]] to her mother might prove harmful, Argo told the reporter that they did not understand how a six-month-old child could be dangerous, taking issue with the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi's]] lack of regard for parental consent. Lomina Argo had curly [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], brown [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and fair skin. They wore large [[Piercing/Legends|earrings]], a headband, and blue-and-white [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]] on at least one occasion.<ref name="Jedi Watch" />

==Behind the scenes==
Lomina Argo first appeared in [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 46|''HoloNet News Vol. 531'' #46]], an issue of the representation of the [[in-universe]] HoloNet News, published on the [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News website]]<ref name="Jedi Watch" /> on [[March 7]], [[2002]].<ref name="HNN47 date">Per {{JCFCite|url=posts/6042498|board=Literature|thread=The Official HoloNetNews Discussion Thread|user=IAmTheDarkSide|date=2002-03-14|time=5:40 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20220914072556/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-holonetnews-discussion-thread.5990764/page-4#post-6042498}}, [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 47|''HoloNet News Vol. 531'' #47]] was released on [[March 14]], [[2002]]. As [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News]] segments were published weekly, and {{HoloNetNewsWeb|46|jediwatch/1337_1.html|Jedi Refuse to Hand Over Baby Ludi}} was published in the issue immediately preceding the forty-seventh edition, it must have been released on [[March 7]] of the same year.</ref> Argo was created by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and [[Paul Ens]],<ref name="Credits">{{HoloNetNewsWeb||credits.html|text=HoloNet News - Credits}}</ref> and they were pictured in an article entitled "Jedi Refuse to Hand Over Baby Ludi,"<ref name="Jedi Watch" /> which was illustrated by artist [[Joe Corroney]].<ref name="Credits" />
